name: SciEntsBank ASAS Multiple Data Augmentation Strategies
population_or_context: Automatic short-answer scoring on the SemEval-2013/SciEntsBank corpus of science questions, reference answers, and student answers.
sample_size: Reported training set: 135 questions, 135 reference answers, and 4969 student answers; total corpus: 331 questions and 10804 student answers across train and test splits.
methods: BERTBASE ASAS classifiers were fine-tuned on original data and several augmented variants: English-Japanese-English back-translation, using correct student answers as additional references, and swapping question/reference/student-answer columns between original and translated examples.
education_context: Science short-answer assessment items with reference answers and student-written responses.
response_origin: Hybrid: real student answers plus machine-translated paraphrase variants and real correct student answers reused as additional reference answers.
rubric_label_space: Two-way, three-way, and five-way ASAS classification labels from the SemEval/SciEntsBank setup.
label_source: Original SciEntsBank/SemEval labels; back-translated variants inherited source-example labels.
generation_model: Baidu machine translation API for English-Japanese-English back-translation; BERTBASE was used as the scorer, not as a generator.
generation_method: Back-translation and structured recombination of original and translated question/reference/student-answer fields.
evaluation_method: Fine-tuned BERTBASE classifiers were evaluated with accuracy, macro-F1, and weighted-F1 on the SciEntsBank unseen-answer test set and compared with prior ASAS systems.
scorer_use: Training augmentation for automatic short-answer scoring; not used as direct validation of a deployed scorer.
real_response_comparator: Yes. Models were evaluated on real held-out SciEntsBank student responses.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: The authors frame ASAS performance as limited by training data scarcity, noting that collecting training answers is tedious and the training split has fewer than 5000 training pairs.
synthetic_data_research_role: Synthetic/paraphrased examples supplement limited ASAS training data and test whether larger augmented training sets improve scorer performance.
reported_metrics: Best combined swap variant improved UA macro-F1 relative to original data by about 2, 2, and 1 points for two-, three-, and five-way classification; Table 3 reports A-origin vs B4-swap-A-B1 UA scores of 0.8055/0.8003/0.8047 vs 0.8277/0.8225/0.8267 for two-way Acc/M-F1/W-F1, 0.7481/0.6795/0.7435 vs 0.7631/0.6934/0.7578 for three-way, and 0.6875/0.5115/0.6857 vs 0.7002/0.5256/0.6998 for five-way.
limitations: The main reported gains are on the unseen-answer test condition; authors identify future work for unseen-question and unseen-domain settings. Augmented examples inherit labels rather than receiving independent human scores.
